product_overview: Notorious.PY is a no-code CustomTkinter interface builder with an integrated AI assistant and Supabase-backed persistence for projects, versions, gallery entries and AI conversations. It supports export to production-ready Python, collaboration features (sharing, cloning), and multi-provider AI generation.
core_goals: Provide a visual no-code builder for CustomTkinter UIs with production-ready Python export.
Integrate an AI assistant that can generate and modify UI designs using multiple providers and persist conversation history.
Persist projects, version snapshots, and gallery entries in Supabase with robust CRUD and clone/publish flows.
Enable user settings and API key storage for AI providers, with secure auth and RLS controls.
Raise production readiness by improving test coverage, performance (fonts/images), logging, and AI quality.

features.name: Project Data Management
features.description: Create, read, update and delete user projects; manage sharing flags and shared-token state so projects can be published or kept private.
user_flows: POST /auth/v1/token with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token -> POST /rest/v1/projects with JWT and body {name, canvas, created_by} -> Receive 201 with project id {projectId} -> GET /rest/v1/projects?id=eq.{projectId} with JWT -> Receive 200 with project record -> PATCH /rest/v1/projects?id=eq.{projectId} with JWT and body {metadata, is_public} -> Receive 204 (updated) -> DELETE /rest/v1/projects?id=eq.{projectId} with JWT -> Receive 204 (deleted)

POST /auth/v1/token with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token -> PATCH /rest/v1/projects?id=eq.{projectId} with JWT and body {is_public=true} for a project not owned by requester -> Receive 403 with RLS/permission error
POST /auth/v1/token with invalid credentials -> Receive 401 Unauthorized -> GET /rest/v1/projects without valid JWT -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

features.name: Versioning and Gallery APIs
features.description: Save project snapshots as versions, publish projects to the public gallery (upsert), and support clone counting for gallery entries.
user_flows: POST /auth/v1/token with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token -> POST /rest/v1/project_versions with JWT and body {project_id:{projectId}, snapshot, metadata} -> Receive 201 with version id {versionId} -> GET /rest/v1/project_versions?project_id=eq.{projectId} with JWT -> Receive 200 with list of versions -> POST /rest/v1/gallery_projects with JWT and body {project_id:{projectId}, title, description} (upsert) -> Receive 200 with gallery record {galleryId} -> POST /rest/v1/rpc/increment_clones_count with JWT and body {gallery_id:{galleryId}} -> Receive 200 with updated clone_count

POST /auth/v1/token with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token -> POST /rest/v1/gallery_projects with JWT and missing required fields (e.g., no title) -> Receive 400 Bad Request with validation error

POST /auth/v1/token with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token -> POST /rest/v1/rpc/increment_clones_count with JWT and invalid gallery_id -> Receive 400 or 404 indicating invalid gallery entry

features.name: AI Conversation and User Settings Persistence
features.description: Persist AI assistant conversations per user/project and store user settings including provider API keys and generation history for reproducible AI runs.
user_flows: POST /auth/v1/token with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token -> POST /rest/v1/ai_conversations with JWT and body {project_id:{projectId}, user_id:{userId}, messages:[...]} -> Receive 201 with conversation id {conversationId} -> GET /rest/v1/ai_conversations?project_id=eq.{projectId}&user_id=eq.{userId} with JWT -> Receive 200 with conversation history -> POST /rest/v1/user_settings with JWT and body {user_id:{userId}, provider_keys:{...}, preferences:{...}} -> Receive 201 (upserted user settings)
POST /auth/v1/token with valid credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token -> POST /rest/v1/user_settings with JWT and invalid API key format -> Receive 400 Validation Error

tech_stack: Supabase Postgres tables (projects, project_versions, gallery_projects, ai_conversations, user_settings)
Frontend-managed service layer for backend data access

features.name: Project Data Management
features.description: CRUD operations for projects and shared token state.

endpoints.description: Fetch project list ordered by last update.
Create project with initial canvas settings.
Update project metadata and sharing flags.

features.name: Versioning and Gallery APIs
features.description: Persist project snapshots and publish/clone gallery entries.

endpoints.description: Save version snapshots for a project.
List and fetch version records by project.
Publish or update a project in gallery (upsert).
Increment clone counter for gallery projects.

features.name: AI Conversation and User Settings Persistence
features.description: Stores AI chats and user preferences/API keys in Supabase tables.

endpoints.description: Fetch conversations scoped by user and project.
Upsert API keys and generation history.

known_limitations.issue: No dedicated backend server exists in this repository; backend tests target Supabase REST/RPC dependencies indirectly.
known_limitations.impact: Backend E2E coverage depends on external Supabase environment configuration and data.
